30.reason: [This truncates the string at the first non-letter character but leaves the rest of the string intact, causing incorrect word extraction. For example, "hello123world" becomes "hello\023world", and fscanf reads it as "hello", but subsequent characters may be processed incorrectly in next iterations. Also, setting s[ou]='\0' does not stop the loop, so later characters may overwrite or corrupt the word.]
